info.name: Telehealth API
info.description:
# Telehealth API — Modular Postman Collection

## Role Badge Legend

Every request name is prefixed with a role badge so you know at a glance who can call it:

| Badge | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `[PUBLIC]` | No authentication required |
| `[ANY]` | Any authenticated user (patient **or** doctor) |
| `[OWN]` | Any authenticated user — but only their **own** resource |
| `[PATIENT]` | Patient role only (`patientToken`) |
| `[DOCTOR]` | Doctor role only (`doctorToken`) |

## How Credentials Flow

1. **Login as Patient** or **Login as Doctor** — the test script auto-captures the JWT and sets `currentToken` + the role-specific token variable.
2. All requests inherit the collection-level `Bearer {{currentToken}}` auth — no manual header copying needed.
3. Use **🔄 Activate Patient Token** or **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** (in the Auth folder) to switch the active role mid-session without re-logging in.

## Collection Variables

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `baseUrl` | API base URL (default: `http://localhost:3000`) |
| `currentToken` | Active JWT — auto-set by login requests |
| `patientToken` | JWT captured from patient login |
| `doctorToken` | JWT captured from doctor login |
| `userId` | Auto-set after login / register |
| `patientId` | Auto-set after patient login |
| `doctorId` | Auto-set after doctor login |
| `appointmentId` | Set after creating an appointment |
| `notificationId` | Set from notification list |
| `rxId` | Set after adding a prescription |
| `blockedSlotId` | Set after blocking a doctor slot |
| `reviewId` | Set after submitting a doctor review |

## Seeded Demo Credentials

| Role | Email | Password |
|---|---|---|
| Doctor | `doctor1@demo.com` | `password123` |
| Doctor | `doctor2@demo.com` | `password123` |
| Patient | `patient1@demo.com` | `password123` |

variable.description: API base URL — change to production URL when deploying
Active JWT token used for all authenticated requests. Auto-set by Login requests.
JWT captured from the most recent Patient Login
JWT captured from the most recent Doctor Login
Current user's ID — auto-set after login
Patient profile ID — auto-set after patient login
Doctor profile ID — auto-set after doctor login
Set from the first notification returned by GET /notifications
Prescription ID — auto-set after adding a prescription
Blocked slot ID — auto-set after blocking a doctor slot
Review ID — auto-set after submitting a doctor review

item.name: 🏥 Health
item.description: Liveness and readiness checks — no auth required.

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp }`. Use this to verify the server is running before making any other requests.

**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Pings the Neon PostgreSQL database with `SELECT 1`. Returns `{ status: 'db connected' }` on success or `500` if the DB is unreachable.

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints plus role-switching helpers.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Register | `PUBLIC` |
| Login | `PUBLIC` |
| Get Current User | `ANY` |
| Role switchers | Helper only |

**Flow:**
1. Run `Login as Patient` or `Login as Doctor` — token auto-captures.
2. Use `🔄 Activate Patient Token` / `🔄 Activate Doctor Token` to switch mid-session.

exec: console.log('✅ Patient registered and token saved');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Register a new patient account. The `role` field must be `patient` or `doctor`.

**Validation:**
- `email` — valid format, max 255 chars
- `password` — min 8 chars
- `firstName` / `lastName` — 1–100 chars
- `role` — enum: `patient` | `doctor`

**Auto-captures on success:** `patientToken`, `currentToken`, `userId`, `patientId`

exec: console.log('✅ Doctor registered and token saved');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Register a new doctor account. Same endpoint as patient registration — role is determined by the `role` field.

**Auto-captures on success:** `doctorToken`, `currentToken`, `userId`, `doctorId`

exec: console.log('✅ Patient token saved → currentToken updated');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Login with seeded patient credentials. Returns a JWT valid for 7 days.

**Seeded demo:** `patient1@demo.com` / `password123`

**Auto-captures on success:** `patientToken`, `currentToken`, `userId`

> After running this, all `[ANY]`, `[OWN]`, and `[PATIENT]` requests will use this token automatically.

exec: console.log('✅ Doctor 1 token saved → currentToken updated');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Login as **Dr. Maria Santos** (Cardiology, PRC-12345). Mon–Fri 09:00–17:00.

**Seeded demo:** `doctor1@demo.com` / `password123`

**Auto-captures on success:** `doctorToken`, `currentToken`, `userId`

> After running this, all `[ANY]`, `[OWN]`, and `[DOCTOR]` requests will use this token automatically.

exec: console.log('✅ Doctor 2 token saved → currentToken updated');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PUBLIC` — No authentication needed.

---

Login as **Dr. Jose Reyes** (General Practice, PRC-67890). Mon/Wed/Fri 08:00–16:00.

**Seeded demo:** `doctor2@demo.com` / `password123`

**Auto-captures on success:** `doctorToken`, `currentToken`, `userId`

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Returns the JWT payload for the current `currentToken`: `id`, `email`, `roles`. Useful for confirming which account is active.

**Returns:** `401` if no token is set.

exec: console.warn('⚠ patientToken is empty — run "[PUBLIC] Login — Patient" first');
// Abort the actual HTTP request — this is a helper only
throw new Error('Role switched to PATIENT — request intentionally aborted');

request.description:
**Helper — not a real API call.** No role required.

---

Sets `currentToken = patientToken` so the next request runs as a patient. The pre-request script swaps the token and intentionally aborts before hitting the server.

**Use when:** You've been testing doctor endpoints and need to switch back to a patient context without re-logging in.

exec: console.warn('⚠ doctorToken is empty — run "[PUBLIC] Login — Doctor" first');
throw new Error('Role switched to DOCTOR — request intentionally aborted');

request.description:
**Helper — not a real API call.** No role required.

---

Sets `currentToken = doctorToken` so the next request runs as a doctor. The pre-request script swaps the token and intentionally aborts before hitting the server.

**Use when:** You're testing `[DOCTOR]` endpoints after having logged in as a patient.

item.name: 👤 Users
item.description:
User profile management and avatar upload.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get User by ID | `ANY` |
| Update User | `OWN` — own profile only |
| Upload Avatar | `OWN` — own avatar only |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Fetch a user by their UUID. Any authenticated user can look up any user by ID.

Uses `{{userId}}` — auto-set after login. To look up a different user, replace `{{userId}}` with their UUID directly.

**Required Role:** `OWN` — Any authenticated user, but only for their **own** profile.

> Attempting to update another user's ID returns `403 Forbidden`.

---

Update profile fields. All fields are optional — only send what you want to change.

**Updatable fields:** `firstName`, `lastName`, `phone`

formdata.description: JPG, PNG, or WebP — max 5 MB

request.description:
**Required Role:** `OWN` — Any authenticated user, but only for their **own** avatar.

> Attempting to upload to another user's ID returns `403 Forbidden`.

---

Upload a profile picture. The file is stored in Cloudinary and the URL is saved to the user record.

**Body:** `form-data` with key `file`
**Accepted types:** `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/webp`
**Max size:** 5 MB

item.name: 🧑‍⚕️ Patients
item.description:
Patient profile management (medical history, emergency contact, etc.).

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get Patient Profile | `ANY` |
| Update Patient Profile | `OWN` — patient, own profile only |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Retrieve a patient's full profile including medical history, blood type, allergies, and emergency contact. Doctors use this to review patient details before a consultation.

Uses `{{patientId}}` — auto-captured after patient login.

**Auto-captures:** `patientId` from response.

**Required Role:** `OWN` — Authenticated user updating **their own** patient profile.

> A doctor attempting to update a patient profile they don't own returns `403 Forbidden`.

---

Update patient medical information. All fields are optional — only send what you want to change.

**Updatable fields:** `dateOfBirth`, `gender`, `bloodType`, `allergies`, `currentMedications`, `medicalHistory`, `emergencyContactName`, `emergencyContactPhone`, `emergencyContactRelationship`

item.name: 👨‍⚕️ Doctors
item.description:
Doctor discovery, profile management, availability, slot blocking, and reviews.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| List Doctors | `ANY` |
| Get All Specializations | `ANY` |
| Get Doctor by ID | `ANY` |
| Update Doctor Profile | `DOCTOR` (own only) |
| Get Availability | `ANY` |
| Update Availability | `DOCTOR` (own only) |
| Get Available Slots | `ANY` |
| Get Blocked Slots | `ANY` |
| Block a Time Slot | `DOCTOR` (own only) |
| Unblock a Time Slot | `DOCTOR` (own only) |
| Get Doctor Reviews | `ANY` |
| Submit Review | `PATIENT` |

> ⚠️ **Route ordering note:** `GET /api/doctors/specializations` is registered before `GET /api/doctors/:id` on the backend to prevent Express treating `"specializations"` as an `:id` param.

exec: // Auto-save first doctor ID for downstream requests

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Paginated list of all doctors. Used by patients to browse and select a doctor before booking.

**Optional query filters:**
- `specialization` — exact match, e.g. `Cardiology`
- `search` — searches name and bio fields
- `page`, `limit` — pagination (default: 1, 20 — max limit: 50)

**Auto-captures:** `doctorId` from the first result for use in downstream requests.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Returns all distinct specialization strings currently in the database, ordered alphabetically.

Used by:
- **Frontend `DoctorFilter` dropdown** — populates the specialization select on the doctor list page. Fetched independently so the dropdown never loses options when a filter is active.
- **AI module** — injects this list into the Gemini prompt so recommendations are constrained to real DB values.

**Response shape:**
```json
["Cardiology", "Dermatology", "General Practice", "Neurology"]
```

> ⚠️ This route must be registered **before** `GET /api/doctors/:id` on the backend to prevent Express treating `"specializations"` as an `:id` param.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Get a single doctor's full profile: specialization, bio, years of experience, consultation fee, and accepting-patients status.

Uses `{{doctorId}}` — auto-set by List Doctors or by a doctor's own login.

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Must be logged in as a doctor AND updating their own profile.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` if a patient attempts this, or if a doctor tries to update another doctor's profile.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if you've been testing as a patient.

---

Update a doctor's profile fields. All fields optional — only send what needs changing.

**Updatable fields:** `bio`, `consultationFee`, `isAcceptingPatients`, `yearsOfExperience`, `specialization`, `licenseNumber`

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Returns the doctor's weekly recurring schedule — which days they work and their start/end times.

**Response shape:**
```json
[
  { "dayOfWeek": "monday", "startTime": "09:00", "endTime": "17:00", "isActive": true }
]
```

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Must be logged in as the doctor whose availability is being updated.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or for a doctor trying to update another doctor's schedule.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Fully replace the doctor's weekly schedule. Body must be an **object** with an `availability` array — **not** a bare array.

**`dayOfWeek` is a number (0–6), not a string:**

| Number | Day |
|---|---|
| 0 | Sunday |
| 1 | Monday |
| 2 | Tuesday |
| 3 | Wednesday |
| 4 | Thursday |
| 5 | Friday |
| 6 | Saturday |

**Validation:**
- `dayOfWeek` — integer `0–6`
- `startTime` / `endTime` — `HH:MM` format
- `isAvailable: false` — marks the day as off even if times are provided

query.description: YYYY-MM-DD — must be a future date

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Returns the list of available 30-minute booking windows for a specific date. The algorithm:
1. Looks up the doctor's weekly schedule for that day of the week
2. Subtracts already-booked appointments
3. Subtracts manually blocked slots
4. Returns remaining open windows

**Required query param:** `date` in `YYYY-MM-DD` format — must be a future date.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

List all manually blocked time slots for this doctor. These slots are excluded from the available-slots algorithm.

**Auto-captures:** `blockedSlotId` from the first result for use in Unblock a Time Slot.

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Must be logged in as the doctor blocking the slot (own schedule only).

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or for a doctor blocking another doctor's slot.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Manually block a specific date + time window so patients cannot book it through the slot picker.

**Fields:**
- `blockedDate` — `YYYY-MM-DD` date of the block (required)
- `startTime` — `HH:MM` start of the blocked window (required)
- `endTime` — `HH:MM` end of the blocked window (required)
- `reason` — optional note for internal reference, max 200 chars

**Auto-captures:** `blockedSlotId`

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Must be logged in as the doctor who owns the blocked slot.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or for a doctor removing another doctor's block.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Remove a previously blocked time slot, making it available for patient booking again.

Uses `{{blockedSlotId}}` — auto-set by **[DOCTOR] Block a Time Slot** or **[ANY] Get Blocked Slots**.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user (patient or doctor).

---

Returns all reviews submitted for a doctor, including patient name, avatar, rating (1–5), comment, and timestamp. Used by the patient-facing doctor profile page to display the review list and star breakdown.

**Response shape:**
```json
[
  {
    "id": "...",
    "rating": 5,
    "comment": "Very thorough and professional.",
    "createdAt": "2026-05-20T10:00:00Z",
    "patient": { "firstName": "Ana", "lastName": "Cruz", "profilePictureUrl": null }
  }
]
```

**Auto-captures:** `reviewId` from the first result.

exec: // Guard: 409 is expected if review already submitted for this appointment
console.warn('⚠ 409 Conflict — a review already exists for this appointment');

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PATIENT` — Only patients can submit reviews.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for doctors.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Patient Token** first if you've been testing as a doctor.

---

Submit a star rating and optional comment for a completed consultation. Displayed on the doctor's public profile.

**Validation rules:**
- `appointmentId` — required, valid UUID; must be a `completed` appointment
- The appointment must belong to the requesting patient (403 if not)
- The appointment must be assigned to the `doctorId` in the URL (400 if mismatch)
- Only **one review per appointment** — returns `409 Conflict` if already reviewed
- `rating` — required, integer `1–5`
- `comment` — optional, max 1000 chars

**Prerequisite flow:**
1. Run **[PATIENT] Create Appointment** → auto-sets `appointmentId`
2. Run **[DOCTOR] Update Appointment Status** with `"status": "completed"`
3. Run this request as patient

**Auto-captures:** `reviewId`

item.name: 📅 Appointments
item.description:
Appointment lifecycle: book, list, view, update status, and cancel.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Create Appointment | `PATIENT` |
| List Appointments | `ANY` (role-filtered results) |
| Get Appointment by ID | `ANY` (own appointment only) |
| Update Status | `DOCTOR` |
| Cancel Appointment | `ANY` (own appointment only) |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PATIENT` — Only patients can book appointments.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` if a doctor attempts to create an appointment.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Patient Token** first if you've been testing as a doctor.

---

Book a new appointment with a doctor. The slot must be within the doctor's available windows and must not conflict with existing bookings.

**Validation:**
- `doctorId` — valid UUID of an existing doctor
- `scheduledAt` — ISO timestamp, must be a **future** datetime
- `durationMinutes` — optional, defaults to `30`
- `reasonForVisit` — optional, max 500 chars

**On success:** Creates the appointment + sends notifications to both patient and doctor via Pusher.

**Auto-captures:** `appointmentId`

exec: // Save first appointment ID if not already set

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user, but results are **automatically filtered by role**:

- **Patient** → sees only their own appointments
- **Doctor** → sees only appointments where they are the assigned doctor

---

Returns a paginated list with the same endpoint for both roles — the service filters based on the JWT.

**Optional `status` filter:** `pending`, `confirmed`, `completed`, `cancelled`

**Auto-captures:** `appointmentId` from the first result (if not already set).

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user who is a **party to the appointment** (the patient who booked it or the doctor it's assigned to).

> Returns `403 Forbidden` if you try to access an appointment you're not part of.

---

Get the full detail of a single appointment, including patient info, doctor info, status, and timestamps.

Uses `{{appointmentId}}` — auto-set by Create Appointment or List Appointments.

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Only the assigned doctor can update an appointment's status.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Change the appointment status. Valid transitions:

| From | To | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `pending` | `confirmed` | Doctor accepts the booking |
| `confirmed` | `completed` | Consultation is done |

**Status values:** `pending` → `confirmed` → `completed`

> Use the **[ANY] Cancel Appointment** DELETE endpoint to cancel instead.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Either the **patient who booked** or the **assigned doctor** can cancel.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` if you try to cancel an appointment you're not a party to.

---

Cancel an appointment. The body `reason` field is optional but recommended for audit purposes.

Sends cancellation notifications to both parties via Pusher.

item.name: 📝 Consultation Notes
item.description:
Consultation notes are scoped to an appointment.

**Base path:** `/api/appointments/:appointmentId/notes`

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get Notes | `ANY` (appointment parties only) |
| Create / Update Notes | `DOCTOR` (assigned doctor only) |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user who is a **party to the appointment** (the patient or the assigned doctor).

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for unrelated users.
> Returns `404` if no notes have been written yet for this appointment.

---

Retrieve the consultation note for a specific appointment. Patients use this to read the doctor's diagnosis and treatment plan after their consultation.

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Only the doctor assigned to this appointment can write notes.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or for a doctor who is not assigned to this specific appointment.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Create or **update** (upsert) consultation notes. Calling this again on the same appointment will overwrite the existing note. All fields are optional.

**Fields:**
- `chiefComplaint` — patient's primary complaint, max 1000 chars
- `diagnosis` — doctor's diagnosis, max 1000 chars
- `notes` — free-text treatment plan, instructions, or remarks, max 3000 chars
- `followUpDate` — optional follow-up date in **`YYYY-MM-DD`** format (e.g. `2026-06-16`) — not an ISO timestamp

**Note:** This endpoint also triggers the appointment status → `completed` transition internally.

item.name: 💊 Prescriptions
item.description:
Prescriptions are scoped to an appointment.

**Base path:** `/api/appointments/:appointmentId/prescriptions`

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get Prescriptions | `ANY` (appointment parties only) |
| Add Prescription | `DOCTOR` (assigned doctor only) |
| Remove Prescription | `DOCTOR` (assigned doctor only) |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user who is a **party to the appointment** (the patient or the assigned doctor).

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for unrelated users.

---

List all prescriptions written for a specific appointment. Patients use this to view their medication list post-consultation.

**Auto-captures:** `rxId` from the first result.

body.raw:
{
  "medicationName": "Aspirin",
  "dosage": "81mg",
  "frequency": "Once daily with meals",
  "duration": "90 days",
  "instructions": "Take with a full glass of water. Do not crush or chew."
}

request.description:
**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Only the doctor assigned to this appointment can add prescriptions.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or unassigned doctors.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Add a single medication to the appointment's prescription list. Multiple prescriptions can be added per appointment by calling this endpoint multiple times.

**Fields:**
- `medicationName` — required
- `dosage` — required, e.g. `81mg`
- `frequency` — required, e.g. `Once daily with meals`
- `duration` — required, e.g. `90 days`
- `instructions` — optional, special handling notes

**Auto-captures:** `rxId`

**Required Role:** `DOCTOR` — Only the doctor assigned to this appointment can remove prescriptions.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for patients or unassigned doctors.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Doctor Token** first if needed.

---

Remove a specific prescription from the appointment.

Uses `{{rxId}}` — auto-set by **[DOCTOR] Add Prescription** or **[ANY] Get Prescriptions for Appointment**.

item.name: 🔔 Notifications
item.description:
In-app notifications (also delivered via Pusher real-time). Created automatically on appointment lifecycle events.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get Notifications | `ANY` (own notifications only) |
| Mark All as Read | `ANY` (own notifications only) |
| Mark Single as Read | `ANY` (own notifications only) |

request.description:
**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user, but only **their own** notifications are returned.

---

Returns the current user's notifications list plus an `unreadCount` summary.

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "notifications": [...],
  "unreadCount": 3
}
```

**Auto-captures:** `notificationId` from the first notification.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user; marks **their own** unread notifications as read.

---

Mark all of the current user's unread notifications as read in a single call. Resets `unreadCount` to `0`.

> ⚠️ **Route ordering note (backend):** This route is registered before `/:id/read` to prevent Express from matching `read-all` as an ID param.

**Required Role:** `ANY` — Any authenticated user; marks **their own** notification as read.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` if you try to mark another user's notification.

---

Mark a single notification as read by ID.

Uses `{{notificationId}}` — auto-set by **[ANY] Get Notifications**.

item.name: 🤖 AI
item.description:
AI-powered doctor recommendations via Gemini.

| Request | Role |
|---|---|
| Get Doctor Recommendations | `PATIENT` only |

exec: // Save first recommended doctorId for downstream booking
console.log('📌 doctorId updated from AI recommendation:', firstDoc.id);

request.description:
**Required Role:** `PATIENT` — Only patients can request AI recommendations.

> Returns `403 Forbidden` for doctors.

> ⚠️ Run **🔄 Activate Patient Token** first if you've been testing as a doctor.

---

Describe symptoms in plain text and receive matched specializations + available doctors in each specialization. Powered by the Gemini API.

**Request:**
```json
{ "symptoms": "string describing symptoms or health concerns" }
```

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "specialization": "Cardiologist",
      "reason": "Based on chest pain and shortness of breath",
      "doctors": [ { "id": "...", "name": "...", "specialization": "..." } ]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Auto-captures:** `doctorId` from the first recommended doctor — immediately ready for **[PATIENT] Create Appointment**.
